data.description:
# e2e editor test `test_editor_batch_library_select_and_author` fails on main (authored criterion not referenced)

## Reproduction Steps
1. From a checkout of `main` (verified at `d42afd880`), with Node/npm available locally:
2. `python -m pytest tests/e2e/test_editor_batch_library.py::test_editor_batch_library_select_and_author`

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** after the editor's batch AUTHOR flow creates a new criterion (`my-new-crit`),
  the batch step's first criterion references it: `authored["batch"]["criteria"][0]["prompt"] == "my-new-crit"`.
- **Actual:** the step still references the pre-existing criteria — `[{'prompt': 'tests'},
  {'prompt': 'ticket-quality', 'when': ...}]`; assertion fails `'tests' == 'my-new-crit'`
  (`tests/e2e/test_editor_batch_library.py:99`).

## Notes
- **Pre-existing on `main`** — reproduces identically with and without the item-15 llm reorg
  (change 238), so it is NOT caused by that change. Discovered while running the full suite for
  ITEM 15 (toll-clock-tier).
- Does not block the gating Gerrit CI (the `not integration and not external` default suite): the
  `e2e` tier self-skips when Node/npm is absent, so the CI image does not exercise it (which is
  how it reached `main`). It DOES fail locally where Node is present.
- Likely in the editor-server batch-author path (`llm/workflow/editor_server.py` /
  `prompt_library` write + criterion-reference wiring), not the prompt reorg.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root-cause why the authored criterion id is not written onto the batch step's first criterion.
- [ ] `test_editor_batch_library_select_and_author` passes locally (with Node present) and in CI.
- [ ] Confirm the `e2e` tier's CI skip behavior is intended (or wire Node into the gate so e2e regressions are caught).
data.title: e2e editor batch-author test fails on main: authored criterion not referenced
